references:
1. Vibe AIGC: A New Paradigm for Content Generation via Agentic Orchestration (https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.04575)
   - Type: methodological_inspiration
   - Justification: Core inspiration paper proposing the Vibe paradigm — agentic orchestration with Meta-Planner to bridge the intent-execution gap in generative AI. VibeMed adapts this framework to the medical and healthcare domain.
   - Usage: Adapt the hierarchical multi-agent orchestration architecture (Meta-Planner, decomposition of user intent into executable agent workflows) from content generation to medical image computing and clinical decision support.
dataset_description: Dataset to be determined during idea generation. Potential datasets for medical agentic orchestration evaluation include: (1) Medical image benchmarks (e.g., BTCV for multi-organ segmentation, BraTS for brain tumor analysis, CheXpert/MIMIC-CXR for chest X-ray interpretation), (2) Clinical workflow datasets with multi-step annotations, (3) Radiology report generation benchmarks. The specific dataset will depend on which clinical workflow domain is selected during the idea generation phase. Since VibeMed is a framework/systems paper, evaluation may involve multiple datasets to demonstrate generalizability across clinical tasks.
